title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B model ranks fourth on LM Arena among open models while being 2-20x smaller than competitors at similar quality. Variants run on single GPUs, mobile phones (2B and 4B), and local machines via LM Studio, with an Apache 2.0 license enabling adoption by sovereign institutions without procurement delays.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma's open models as a path to ownership for sovereign institutions. The 31B model ranks fourth among open models on LM Arena while running 2-20x smaller than competitors at comparable quality, and smaller variants run on single GPUs, on phones (2B and 4B), and locally through LM Studio. The Apache 2.0 license is the central point: it lets institutions adopt the models without procurement delays.
